findings.claim: Automattic acquired Beeper in April 2024 for a reported $125 million, with all 27 Beeper team members transitioning to Automattic
Automattic acquired Texts.com in October 2023 for $50 million, founded by Kishan Bagaria, making the combined messaging investment $175 million
Eric Migicovsky (Pebble founder, Beeper co-founder) joined Automattic as Head of Messaging in April 2024 upon the Beeper acquisition
Migicovsky later stepped down from the Beeper CEO role and launched Core Devices (Pebble revival) in 2025; Kishan Bagaria (Texts.com founder) took over as CEO of Beeper under Automattic
Matt Mullenweg stated 'private, free, encrypted, open-source communication is a fundamental human right' and positioned messaging as Automattic's third pillar alongside publishing and commerce
Beeper is built on the Matrix protocol, using 'puppeting' bridges via the open-source mautrix libraries (Go/Python) to translate proprietary chat protocols into Matrix events
Beeper uses a custom high-performance Matrix homeserver called Hungryserv, optimized for bridging thousands of accounts simultaneously
Beeper introduced Bridge v2 (Megabridge) in 2024, shifting from one-process-per-network to a modular system supporting client-side/on-device bridging, primarily in the mautrix-go library
In mid-2025 Beeper transitioned from cloud-based bridges to on-device connections, preserving end-to-end encryption by keeping decryption keys on the user's device
Beeper had a waitlist of over 466,000 users and approximately 115,000 active beta users before the waitlist was removed on April 9, 2024
Beeper Mini (iMessage for Android) saw over 100,000 downloads within its first 48 hours in December 2023 before being blocked by Apple
The Texts.com brand has been deprecated and all operations consolidated under the Beeper brand, with the new Beeper Desktop app built on Texts.com's local-first foundation
Beeper operates within Automattic's 'Other Bets' division, maintaining operational independence while utilizing parent company resources

Beeper 2025 pricing: Free tier (up to 5 connected accounts), Beeper Plus ($9.99/month for 10 accounts, Send Later, Incognito Mode, AI voice transcription), Beeper Plus Plus ($49.99/month for unlimited accounts)
Beeper supports 14+ chat networks including WhatsApp, Signal, Telegram, Slack, Discord, Instagram, iMessage (macOS), and Google Messages (RCS)

Beeper integrated Model Context Protocol (MCP) into its Desktop API (v4.1.169+), allowing AI assistants like Claude to search, summarize, and draft across all connected chat networks locally on localhost:23373
The DOJ's March 2024 antitrust lawsuit against Apple (United States v. Apple Inc., No. 2:24-cv-04055) specifically cited Apple's blocking of Beeper Mini as evidence of anticompetitive behavior
Beeper leverages the EU Digital Markets Act (DMA) for iOS system-level interoperability access rather than attempting to reverse-engineer iMessage again post-acquisition
Automattic's long-term strategy includes integrating unified chat into WooCommerce, allowing small businesses to manage customer queries from 14+ social networks within a WordPress dashboard

Beeper is the primary maintainer and sponsor of the mautrix open-source organization (github.com/mautrix), providing industry-standard Matrix bridging libraries, with bridges shifting from Python to Go in 2024
Beeper supports self-hosting via bbctl (Beeper Bridge Control) CLI tool using a custom Appservice Websocket protocol, allowing local bridges without a public IP
Beeper utilizes Matrix Spec Changes (MSCs) including MSC2409 (push read receipts/typing to AppServices) and MSC3202 (Encrypted AppServices) plus custom event types like com.beeper.voice_message
Mullenweg stated he has 'zero interest in fighting with Apple' and instead aims to build a superior open-source messaging client for networks that support interoperability

key_insights: Automattic invested $175M to acquire both universal messaging startups (Texts.com + Beeper), consolidating the market and creating a dedicated messaging division led by serial entrepreneur Eric Migicovsky, with Kishan Bagaria later taking over as CEO -- signaling this is not a side project but a core strategic bet comparable in importance to WordPress and WooCommerce.

The technical architecture is built on the open Matrix protocol with Beeper as primary sponsor of the mautrix bridge ecosystem, shifting in 2025 from cloud-based to on-device bridging for true end-to-end encryption -- combined with MCP integration for AI assistants and a freemium SaaS model ($9.99-$49.99/month), this positions Beeper as infrastructure for the open messaging web rather than just another chat app.

Beeper's conflict with Apple over iMessage became a centerpiece of the DOJ antitrust lawsuit and EU DMA enforcement, giving Automattic regulatory leverage that a standalone startup could never muster -- the company is betting that regulatory tailwinds (DMA-mandated interoperability, DOJ pressure) will force open the walled gardens that make a universal inbox viable long-term.
gaps: Exact active user counts for Beeper post-public launch (after April 2024 waitlist removal) and throughout 2025 are not publicly disclosed
Revenue figures for the messaging division are not broken out -- Automattic is private and does not publish segment-level financials
Detailed terms of Eric Migicovsky's departure from the Head of Messaging role and the exact timeline of the leadership transition to Kishan Bagaria lack primary source confirmation
Whether Beeper has achieved product-market fit or remains in investment/growth mode without profitability targets is unclear
Specifics on WooCommerce-Beeper integration roadmap (unified business messaging dashboard) remain speculative with no announced shipping timeline
